description: Table itemtypes du mod TCP (colonnes documentees via le D2R Data Guide).
source: Colonnes du .txt (mod TCP) ; types/descriptions du D2R Data Guide (guide/).

columns.name: ItemType
columns.description: This is a reference field to define the Item Type name

columns.name: Code
columns.description: Defines the unique pointer for this Item Type, which is used by various data files

columns.name: Repair
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item can be repaired by an NPC in the shop UI. If equals 0, then the item cannot be repaired

columns.name: Body
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item can be equipped by a character (Also uses the

columns.name: Shoots
columns.description: Points to the index of another Item Type as the required equipped Item Type to be used as ammo

columns.name: Quiver
columns.description: Points to the index of another Item Type as the required equipped Item Type to be used as this ammo's weapon

columns.name: Reload
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item (considered ammo in this case) will be automatically transferred from the inventory to the required

columns.name: ReEquip
columns.description: If equals 1, then the item in the inventory will replace a matching equipped item if that equipped item was destroyed. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: AutoStack
columns.description: If equals 1, then if the player picks up a matching Item Type, then they will try to automatically stack together. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: Magic
columns.description: If equals 1, then this item will always have the Magic quality (unless it is a Quest item). If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: Rare
columns.description: If equals 1, then this item can spawn as a Rare quality. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: Normal
columns.description: If equals 1, then this item will always have the Normal quality. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: Beltable
columns.description: If equals 1, then this item can be placed in the character's belt slots. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: TreasureClass
columns.description: If equals 1, then allow this Item Type to be used in default treasure classes. If equals 0, then ignore this

columns.name: Rarity
columns.description: Determines the chance for the item to spawn with stats, when created as a random Weapon/Armor/Misc item. Used in the following formula:

columns.name: StaffMods
columns.description: Determines if the Item Type should have class specific item skill modifiers. Grants skills to the class listed using the

columns.name: Class
columns.description: Determines if this item should be useable only by a specific class. Referenced from the

columns.name: VarInvGfx
columns.description: Tracks the number of inventory graphics used for this item type. This number much match the number of

columns.name: StorePage
columns.description: Uses a code to determine which UI tab page on the NPC shop UI to display this Item Type, such as after it is sold to the NPC. Referenced from the
